expectations.text: Response checks for critical tools (bun, node, git)
expectations.evidence: Critical Tools table checks bun (1.3.6), node (v25.2.1), and git (2.50.1) with version comparisons against minimum required versions.
Uses [PASS], [FAIL], [WARN], and [INFO] status indicators throughout. Examples: '[PASS]' for bun/node/git, '[FAIL]' for missing package.json, '[WARN]' for missing .env files.
Project Configuration Files table includes '.env / .env.local' with status '[WARN] Missing' and '.env.example' with status '[WARN] Missing'.

expectations.text: Response provides remediation steps for failures
expectations.evidence: 'What to do next' section provides numbered remediation steps: install Wrangler instructions, set up app project instructions, create .env.local file, and a re-run command for verification.

expectations.text: Response detects project stack from package.json
expectations.evidence: Stack Detection section states: 'No package.json found, so stack detection could not run. Once you have an app project with a package.json, the following components would be detected: ElysiaJS, Drizzle ORM, tRPC, Zod, Neon, TanStack libraries, Hono, and others.' This demonstrates the feature exists even though no package.json was present.
